bio: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Jr. was part of the Palmer, Washington County, Kansas Stuenkel family and later died in Fremont, Dodge County, Nebraska. A Find A Grave memorial matching his 1918-1976 dates preserves his burial record and confirms the Kansas/Nebraska family footprint.
records: Find A Grave memorial #42870488 for Edmund Henry Stuenkel Jr.
Dugan Funeral Chapel obituary for Lydia N. Stuenkel, noting her 1952 marriage to Edmund and their three children.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Jr.
Dugan Funeral Chapel: Lydia N. Stuenkel obituary

funFacts: His wife Lydia's obituary says they married on 17 June 1952 and had three children, explaining the later Fremont, Nebraska household around a Kansas-born Stuenkel.

bio: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Sr. was born in Sterling, Nebraska, and spent his later life in Washington County, Kansas. His Find A Grave memorial places his burial in Washington Cemetery, Washington County, Kansas, tying this branch to the north-central Kansas Lutheran/German-settler community.
records: Find A Grave memorial #98758297 for Edmund Henry Stuenkel Sr.
FamilySearch Ancestors profile LR32-964 for Christine M. Stuenkel, naming Edmund and siblings in August and Christine's family.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Sr.

funFacts: FamilySearch's profile for his mother Christine lists Edmund among a large sibling group that included Henry August, Frederick “Fritz,” Julius, Harry John Herman, and Christina Stuenkel.

bio: Bertha A. Gabbert married Edmund H. Stuenkel and is recorded in the Palmer, Washington County, Kansas area. Her burial is recorded at Saint Johns Lutheran Cemetery in Palmer, reflecting the family’s continued connection to German Lutheran communities in Kansas.
records: Find A Grave memorial #98329539 for Bertha A. Gabbert Stuenkel.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Bertha A. Gabbert Stuenkel

funFacts: Her memorial's 1881–1935 dates mean Edmund Jr. was still a teenager when his mother died at age 53.

bio: Dietrich Louis August Stuenkel appears in later records as August Louis “Gus” Stuenkel of Linn, Washington County, Kansas. A genealogy summary says he died suddenly at the family home in Linn and was a well-known local figure.
funFacts: He and Christine M. Stuenkel were reported to have at least 6 sons and 3 daughters.
An obituary summary says that on the evening he died, he had been helping Emil Schwerdtfeger wire a recently remodeled property.
records: Find A Grave memorial #113231341 for August Louis Stuenkel.
Find A Grave memorial/obituary summary describing August L. Stuenkel's sudden death after an evening of wiring work.

citations.label: Find A Grave: August Louis Stuenkel

bio: Maria Dorothea Christine Stuenkel appears in FamilySearch/Find A Grave-indexed material as Christine M. Stuenkel. Those records identify her parents as Friedrich Heinrich Konrad Stuenkel and Sophie Charlotte Frerking, and say she married August L. Stuenkel on 25 July 1878 in Lafayette County, Missouri.
funFacts: The FamilySearch summary reports that Christine and August L. Stuenkel had at least 6 sons and 3 daughters.
FamilySearch's child list shows the family preserving German names while also using American nicknames such as Frederick “Fritz” Stuenkel.
records: FamilySearch ancestor profile LR32-964.
Find A Grave memorial #113231470 for Christine M. Stuenkel.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Christine M. Stuenkel

bio: Heinrich Emil “Henry” Gabbert was a German-born settler who lived in Linn, Washington County, Kansas. Search-indexed cemetery and genealogy records identify him as a farmer, husband of Caroline Schmidt, and father of several children including Bertha A. Stuenkel.
funFacts: Find A Grave places him in Zion Lutheran Cemetery at Linn, Kansas; search snippets report a death-date discrepancy of 21 July 1917 versus the roster’s 23 July 1917.
Search-indexed cemetery and genealogy summaries say Henry and Caroline married in 1876, anchoring Bertha Gabbert's family in Linn before Bertha's 1881 birth.
immigration: Born in Prussia/Germany and settled in Washington County, Kansas; no ship or arrival year was confirmed in the sources found.
records: Find A Grave memorial #113812084 for Heinrich Emil “Henry” Gabbert.
Find A Grave/MyHeritage-indexed summary for Heinrich Emil Gabbert reporting his 1876 marriage to Caroline Schmidt.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Heinrich Emil Gabbert

bio: Caroline Schmidt was the wife of Heinrich Emil “Henry” Gabbert in the German Lutheran community around Linn, Washington County, Kansas. Find A Grave-indexed records place her burial with the Gabbert family at Zion Lutheran Cemetery in Linn.
funFacts: The Gabbert family search result names children including Bertha A. Stuenkel, Emilie O. Timme, Heinrich Henry Peter T. Gabbert, and Emil William Gabbert.
Find A Grave gives her full birth date as 11 December 1848, narrowing the roster's month-only December 1848 birth entry.
records: Find A Grave memorial #113812127 for Caroline Gabbert.

bio: A search-indexed Find A Grave memorial matches Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel with the 1808-1876 dates in the roster and a Lafayette County, Missouri connection. Because the roster has an apparent duplicate for the same name and dates, this same cautious record note is attached to both IDs.
records: Find A Grave memorial #27728629 for Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel

funFacts: His Lafayette County years placed him in a Missouri county where German immigrants became a large part of communities such as Concordia, Emma, Wellington, Napoleon, Higginsville, Mayview, and Lexington.

bio: A search-indexed Find A Grave memorial matches Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel with the 1808-1876 dates in the roster and a Lafayette County, Missouri connection. Because the roster has an apparent duplicate for the same name and dates, this same cautious record note is attached to both IDs.
records: Find A Grave memorial #27728629 for Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel

funFacts: His Lafayette County years placed him in a Missouri county where German immigrants became a large part of communities such as Concordia, Emma, Wellington, Napoleon, Higginsville, Mayview, and Lexington.

bio: WikiTree lists Louisa Elizabeth Scharnhorst Stuenkel as a daughter of Louis Scharnhorst and Louise Gasdorff. The same profile records her death in 1869 and burial in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery, Concordia, Lafayette County, Missouri.
records: WikiTree profile Scharnhorst-117.
Find A Grave memorial #35135471, cited by WikiTree.
funFacts: Her 1869 death came just one year after nearby Concordia was platted, during the German-settler era in Lafayette County.

bio: Sophie Charlotte Frerking appears in her mother Dorothea Sprengel Frerking’s 1850 census discussion as Charlotte Stuenkle, age 27, born in Germany. She was living in District 46, Lafayette County, Missouri with husband Frederic Stuenkle, two Missouri-born children, and her mother Mary/Dorothea Frerking on the Stuenkel farm.
immigration: Her widowed mother Dorothea emigrated to America with her children in 1845 aboard the ship General Washington from Bremen to New Orleans; Sophie Charlotte is documented in Missouri by the 1850 census.
records: 1850 U.S. census entry cited on WikiTree for Frederic and Charlotte Stinkle/Stuenkle in Lafayette County, Missouri.
Frerking family immigration record via the ship General Washington, cited on WikiTree.
Esperke village history and chapel context for Sophie Charlotte Frerking's birthplace.
funFacts: Her birthplace Esperke was a Leine-area village first recorded in 1268 and remembered for a small Gothic chapel more than 700 years old.

bio: Johann Dietrich Frerking married Dorothea Maria Sprengel on 29 November 1811 in Esperke, Hanover. WikiTree records that he died on 15 July 1838 and was buried on 18 July 1838 in Esperke, before Dorothea and their children emigrated to Missouri.
records: WikiTree profile Frerking-57 for marriage, death, and burial notes.
Esperke village history and chapel context for Johann Dietrich Frerking's marriage and burial place.
funFacts: His documented marriage, death, and burial all sit in Esperke, a Leine-area village first recorded in 1268 and remembered for a small Gothic chapel more than 700 years old.

bio: Dorothea Maria Sprengel was baptized in Esperke in April 1792 and married Johann Dietrich Frerking there in 1811. After Johann’s death, she emigrated with her children to America and was living in Lafayette County, Missouri by the 1850 census, in the household of daughter Sophie Charlotte and son-in-law Frederic Stuenkle. She died in 1868 and was buried in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery in Concordia.
immigration: Dorothea emigrated in 1845 aboard the ship General Washington, departing Bremen and arriving at New Orleans on 24 November 1845.
records: FamilySearch passenger-list record QKNP-MJZR cited by WikiTree.
1850 U.S. census record MDZ8-FFP cited by WikiTree.
Find A Grave memorial #34625151 cited by WikiTree.
funFacts: Her 1845 arrival brought the widowed Frerking family through New Orleans, described in her WikiTree profile as the third-largest U.S. city at the time.

bio: WikiTree records Louis/Ludwig Scharnhorst as born in 1792 and dying in 1879 at Concordia, Lafayette County, Missouri. His burial is listed in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery, tying the Scharnhorst family into the Concordia Lutheran cemetery network.
records: WikiTree profile Scharnhorst-89.
Find A Grave memorial #35135791 cited by WikiTree.
funFacts: Concordia was platted in 1868 and had many early German settlers, so Ludwig's 1879 burial there places him among the community's first German-settler generation.

bio: Louise Gasdorff Scharnhorst is recorded on WikiTree as born in 1801 and dying on 17 October 1877. Her burial is listed in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery, Concordia, Lafayette County, Missouri, alongside other German Lutheran families in this branch.
records: WikiTree profile Gasdorff-1.
Find A Grave memorial #35135768 cited by WikiTree.
funFacts: She lived long enough to see Concordia formally platted in 1868 and died in the German-settler community less than a decade later.

bio: WikiTree’s Stuenkel surname page lists Johann Ludwig Stuenkel with a 5 March 1776 birth in Metel, Neustadt, Hanover, and an 18 April 1853 death in the Neustadt/Hannover area. The same surname page places him immediately among the Metel-to-Missouri Stuenkel profiles, including Fredrich Heinrich Konrad Stuenkel and Conrad Dietrich Friedrich Stuenkel.
records: WikiTree Stuenkel surname index entry for Johann Ludwig Stuenkel (profile Stuenkel-50).
Search-indexed Ancestry profile for Johann Friedrick Ludwig Stuenkel, Margarete Katharina Dorothea Mahler, and their children.

citations.label: Ancestry index: Johann Friedrick Ludwig Stuenkel

funFacts: Search-indexed genealogy records report that he and Margarete Katharina Dorothea Mahler had at least nine children, making the Metel Stuenkels a large sibling network before later Missouri migration.

bio: Search-indexed genealogy material identifies Margaret Katherine Dorothea Mahler as the wife of Johann Friedrick Ludwig Stuenkel and mother of a large Metel-area Stuenkel family; the public indexes vary slightly from the roster by giving a 1783 birth and Brase/Basse-area wording, so the exact village/date should be checked against original church records.
funFacts: Her Basse-area origin sits in an old Leine village landscape where Basse's church was founded around 1100 and the Reformation was ordered in 1543.
records: Search-indexed Ancestry profile for Margaret Katherine Dorothea Mahler and Johann Friedrick Ludwig Stuenkel.
Basse village history for local parish and Reformation context.

citations.label: Ancestry index: Margaret Katherine Dorothea Mahler

bio: Dorothea Maria Sprengel Frerking's WikiTree profile names Johann Heinrich Sprengel and Anna Margareta Raben as her parents, but it gives Johann Heinrich's birth year as 1754 rather than the roster's 1765, so his exact dates need original Esperke church-record confirmation.
funFacts: This branch has a useful source conflict: public WikiTree links him to Dorothea but disagrees with the roster's birth year, a clue for future church-book checking rather than a fact to smooth over.
records: WikiTree profile for Dorothea Maria Sprengel Frerking naming Johann Heinrich Sprengel as her father.

bio: Frerking.net lists Anna Margareta Raben as born 10 November 1766 in Niedernstocken and dying 10 August 1831 in Esperke; Dorothea Sprengel's WikiTree profile also names Anna Margareta Raben as Dorothea's mother.
funFacts: Her life connects neighboring Leine villages: Niedernstöcken lies just west of Esperke, where her daughter Dorothea was baptized and married.
records: Frerking.net person page for Anna Margareta Raben.
WikiTree profile for Dorothea Maria Sprengel Frerking naming Anna Margareta Raben as her mother.
Niedernstöcken and Esperke local-history summaries for the village context.

bio: Frerking.net gives a sparse but useful public listing for Johann Diederich Sprengel with 1726–1787 dates; no fuller open record was found during this pass.
records: Frerking.net person page for Johann Diederich Sprengel.

bio: A search-indexed Ancestry genealogy profile matches Ilse Elisabeth Fasterling with the 1759-1788 Berel, Lower Saxony dates in this branch. The Berel local-history article notes that the village is among the earliest documented places in the Wolfenbüttel district and Lower Saxony, giving useful context for this Fasterling/Vornkahl line.
funFacts: Berel is documented under early forms such as Perlohen/Berle and is described as one of the earliest-mentioned localities in the Wolfenbüttel district.
records: Ancestry public genealogy index for Ilse Elisabeth Fasterling, 1759-1788.
Berel local-history article for village context.

bio: A public OFB/Genealogy.net listing surfaces an Ilse Catharine Bühring in Groß Himstedt with a 12 January 1723 birth, close to but not identical with this GEDCOM person’s 1720 estimate. Because the date differs, this should be treated as a candidate Bührig/Bühring lead rather than a confirmed identity.
funFacts: Groß Himstedt is now part of Söhlde in Landkreis Hildesheim and lies near Berel, matching the broader geography of this branch.
records: OFB/Genealogy.net Celle-family-database surname listing for Bühring, Ilse Catharine; candidate only because of date conflict.
Groß Himstedt local-history article for place context.

bio: The Mandelsloh online family database is directly relevant to this branch because its parish coverage includes Amedorf and its church books begin in October 1678. Search-indexed material points to Anne Catharine/Catrina Barbara Schünhoff as a likely spelling variant for Cath Barbara Schumhoff, born in Mandelsloh-area records in 1688 and dying in Amedorf in 1746.
funFacts: The Mandelsloh OFB warns that pre-1749 baptisms often omit mothers’ names, an important caution when using these early Amedorf records.
records: Familiendatenbank Kirchspiel Mandelsloh for Amedorf parish coverage and source limitations.
Search-indexed Mandelsloh/OFB lead for Anne Catharine Barbara Schünhoff / Catrina Barbara Schünhoff.

citations.label: Ancestry index: Johann Philipp von Gilten

bio: A search-indexed Ancestry profile matches Johann Philipp von Gilten with birth on 21 June 1681 in Amedorf and death on 6 June 1758 in Amedorf. The same indexed profile names his parents as Hinrich Adam von Gilten and J. Catrina Gerdrut Degener and his spouse as Anne Catharine Schuenhoff.
records: Ancestry public genealogy index for Johann Philipp von Gilten, 1681-1758.
Familiendatenbank Kirchspiel Mandelsloh for Amedorf parish context.

citations.label: Ancestry index: Johann Philipp von Gilten

bio: A public Ancestry index for her son Johann Philipp von Gilten names J. Catrina Gerdrut Degener as Johann Philipp’s mother. No independent open record for her exact 1659-1709 dates was confirmed in this pass, so this entry relies on that secondary indexed family-tree connection and should be checked against Mandelsloh/Amedorf parish registers.
records: Ancestry public genealogy index for Johann Philipp von Gilten naming J. Catrina Gerdrut Degener as mother.
Familiendatenbank Kirchspiel Mandelsloh for parish-record context and cautions.

citations.label: Ancestry index: Johann Philipp von Gilten

bio: A public Ancestry index for Johann Philipp von Gilten names Hinrich Adam von Gilten as Johann Philipp’s father, matching this branch’s Heinrich Adam von Gilten. No separate open record for Heinrich Adam’s exact 1649-1703 dates was confirmed, so the safest enrichment is the sourced parent-child lead plus the Amedorf parish context.
records: Ancestry public genealogy index for Johann Philipp von Gilten naming Hinrich Adam von Gilten as father.
Familiendatenbank Kirchspiel Mandelsloh for Amedorf parish-record context.

citations.label: Ancestry index: Johann Philipp von Gilten

bio: WikiTree and Geneal-Tree indexed material identify Anna Maria Katharina Breckheimer as born on 25 February 1677 in Flörsheim am Main and dying there on 3 December 1748. The same records place her as daughter of Christian Georg Breckheimer and Juliana Hart and wife of Johann Kaspar Müller, with a 3 May 1696 marriage in Flörsheim.
funFacts: The indexed family summaries list a large Müller household; one summary says Anna Maria and Johann Kaspar had thirteen children.
records: WikiTree profile Breckheimer-6 for Anna Maria Katharina Breckheimer.
Geneal-Tree entry for Anna Maria Breckheimer.

bio: Geneal-Tree and related indexed sources list Juliana Hart as born on 15 October 1645 in Flörsheim am Main and dying there on 16 September 1720. The same search-indexed material connects her as the wife of Christian Georg Breckheimer and mother of Anna Maria Breckheimer.
records: Geneal-Tree entry for Juliane Hart of Flörsheim.
Search-indexed Ancestry/Geneal-Tree material for the Hart-Breckheimer family.

bio: Geneal-Tree and search-indexed Ancestry material identify Christian Georg Breckheimer as born about 1635 in Wicker/Flörsheim-area records and dying on 22 July 1696 in Flörsheim am Main. The indexed summaries connect him to wife Juliana Hart and daughter Anna Maria Katharina Breckheimer.
records: Geneal-Tree entry for Christian Georg Breckheimer.
Ancestry public genealogy index for Christian Georg Breckheimer.

bio: GEDBAS indexes Johann Kaspar Müller of Flörsheim am Main with birth on 3 August 1673 and death on 15 January 1729. Search-indexed summaries identify him as a Zöllner, or customs officer, and as the husband of Anna Maria Breckheimer, whom he married in Flörsheim on 3 May 1696.
funFacts: His occupation, Zöllner, points to a toll/customs role rather than farming, adding an occupational detail to the Flörsheim Müller line.
records: GEDBAS entry for Johann Kaspar Müller.
Search-indexed family summaries for the Johann Kaspar Müller-Anna Maria Breckheimer household.

bio: The online summaries for Anna Maria Breckheimer and Johann Kaspar Müller list a son Johann Peter/Petrus Müller baptized or born on 19 April 1700 in Flörsheim and dying on 28 October 1763. Because German and Latin church records often interchange Peter/Petrus, this is a credible match to the GEDCOM’s Johannes Petrus Müller, but the exact parish entry should still be checked.
records: WikiTree/Geneal-Tree indexed summaries for the children of Anna Maria Breckheimer and Johann Kaspar Müller.
GEDBAS entry for Johann Kaspar Müller, father in the same Flörsheim family group.

bio: A Geneal-Tree search result for Johann Heinrich Müller gives the same Flörsheim dates found in this GEDCOM person—6 July 1636 and 9 November 1694—but the given name differs from Johann Nicol. Treat this as a useful same-place/same-date Müller lead, not as proof that the records are for the same man, until the Flörsheim parish entry is reviewed.
funFacts: This is a good example of why early German research needs spelling and given-name reconciliation rather than automatic merging.
records: Geneal-Tree entry/search result for Johann Heinrich Müller with matching 1636-1694 Flörsheim dates; candidate lead only.
